seo.meta_title: Logto vs Supabase Auth for Next.js App Router (2026): Which Auth Tool Wins? (Updated Week 24, 2026)
seo.meta_description: Logto and Supabase Auth are both capable auth tools for Next.js  –  but they're not interchangeable. I compare Medium vs Easy learning curves, pricing models, and which team each tool actually fits.
seo.og_title: Logto vs Supabase Auth — Which Next.js Auth Tool Wins?
seo.og_description: Choosing auth for your Next.js app? Logto and Supabase Auth compared: Free – $599/mo vs Free – $75/mo, 20 minutes vs 15 minutes setup.

hero.headline: Logto vs Supabase Auth for Next.js Authentication
hero.subheadline: An honest comparison of Logto and Supabase Auth — pricing, DX, setup time, and which one we recommend for Next.js App Router projects in 2026.

quick_summary: Putting Logto and Supabase Auth through the same Next.js project reveals a clear pattern: Logto handles server components, middleware, and edge runtime better. The short version: the 7/10 score reflects real-world testing. Supabase Auth is still a capable option, especially if you care about Free – $75/mo.
verdict_text: Logto is the safer bet for Next.js projects. Its 20 minutes setup time and medium learning curve mean you're productive faster. Supabase Auth isn't far behind  –  especially if you need yes, 50,000 mau included in free tier  –  but Logto has the edge where it counts for App Router development.

structured_data_points.label: Free Tier
structured_data_points.t2: Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier

pros: Built-in management API and admin console
Support for social sign-in, MFA, and passwordless auth
User-friendly admin dashboard for non-technical team members
cons: Smaller community and fewer third-party integrations
Next.js SDK is less mature with fewer examples
Self-hosted version requires significant infrastructure setup

pros: Extremely generous free tier with 50,000 monthly active users
Integrated with Supabase database, storage, and realtime subscriptions
Built-in support for email, phone, OAuth, and magic links
cons: Tightly coupled to the Supabase PostgreSQL ecosystem
Less focused on auth-only use cases - it's part of a larger platform
Organization and team management features are less mature than Clerk
RLS policies can become complex to manage at scale

faq.question: Should I choose Logto or Supabase Auth for my Next.js app?
faq.answer: It depends on your specific needs. Logto scores 7/10 for Next.js integration with a Medium learning curve. Supabase Auth scores 7/10 with a Easy learning curve. For most projects, we recommend Logto because of its stronger DX and better alignment with App Router patterns. However, if Yes, up to 5,000 MAU or Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier is a deciding factor, the cheaper option may serve you well.

faq.question: Is Logto or Supabase Auth more affordable?
faq.answer: Logto starts at Free – $599/mo with a free tier of Yes, up to 5,000 MAU. Supabase Auth starts at Free – $75/mo with a free tier of Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier. The total cost of ownership depends on your MAU (monthly active users). At lower volumes, the free tiers of both are sufficient. As you scale, Supabase Auth offers better value at scale. Factor in hidden costs like enterprise features, SSO, and database infrastructure when comparing.

faq.question: Is Logto or Supabase Auth easier to set up with Next.js?
faq.answer: Logto has a Medium learning curve with an estimated setup time of 20 minutes. Supabase Auth has a Easy learning curve with an estimated setup time of 15 minutes. Supabase Auth is faster to set up, but ease of integration also depends on your team's familiarity with the framework and whether you want pre-built UI components or prefer to build your own.

faq.question: Can I use Logto and Supabase Auth together?
faq.answer: Using both Logto and Supabase Auth in the same Next.js application is not recommended. They would create separate session management systems, conflicting middleware, and competing cookies. Choose one auth provider and stick with it. Migrating between auth providers later is possible but requires user data export and rebuilding authentication flows.

faq.question: Which has better developer experience: Logto or Supabase Auth?
faq.answer: Logto has a Medium learning curve, a Next.js integration score of 7/10, and a user rating of 4.1★. Supabase Auth has a Easy learning curve, 7/10, and 4.4★. On paper, Logto leads in DX. In practice, your specific requirements around pre-built components, customization, and team expertise matter just as much. Try both free tiers to see what clicks.
